game_name: Lufia II: Rise of the Sinistrals

sub_pixel_system.notes: No sub-pixel system documentation found in available sources

special_mechanics.name: Turn-Based Dungeon Movement
special_mechanics.description: Motion in dungeons is turn-based; monsters do not move unless the player moves first. This affects movement pacing and timing mechanics

special_mechanics.name: Tool-Based Interaction
special_mechanics.description: Maxim gains dungeon-specific tools (bow and arrow, bombs, hookshot) that stun monsters temporarily and interact with obstacles

special_mechanics.name: World Map vs Dungeon Movement
special_mechanics.description: Characters walk around overworld map with random monster encounters; dungeon movement is grid-based and turn-based with visible monsters that can be avoided

special_mechanics.name: Transportation Mechanics
special_mechanics.description: Multiple forms of faster transportation available: warping spell, submarine (modified boat), and airship/blimp

special_mechanics.name: Ancient Cave Roguelike
special_mechanics.description: 99-floor randomly generated dungeon where characters are downgraded to zero experience and stripped of equipment; procedurally generated layout each entry

tier_notes: Tier 2 - Empirical/Qualitative. Game is primarily an RPG with puzzle emphasis rather than action-platformer. Available documentation focuses on game design, features, and content rather than precise movement physics parameters. No specific velocity, acceleration, gravity, or sub-pixel data documented in provided sources.
sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - Lufia II: Rise of the Sinistrals (debug menu and gameplay mechanics)
Wikipedia - Lufia II: Rise of the Sinistrals (gameplay mechanics and features)
